faqs.question: What is the difference between MM32W373 and MM32W073?
faqs.answer: The MM32W373PF offers twice the memory of the MM32W073PF: (1) Flash - 128KB vs 64KB, providing more space for application code and firmware updates. (2) RAM - 16KB vs 8KB, enabling larger buffers and more complex data processing. (3) ADC channels - 24 vs 16 channels for more sensor inputs. (4) Both share the same BLE radio and core, so RF performance and power consumption are identical. Choose MM32W373 when: Application code exceeds 60KB, multiple BLE connections required, complex protocol stacks needed, or OTA dual-bank updates required. Choose MM32W073 for: Simple sensor applications, cost-sensitive designs, or when memory requirements are modest. The price difference is typically 15-20%, making the MM32W373 a cost-effective upgrade when more memory is needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for memory size analysis and MM32W series selection guidance.

faqs.question: How many simultaneous BLE connections can MM32W373 support?
faqs.answer: The MM32W373PF can support up to 8 simultaneous BLE connections when operating as a central device (master). As a peripheral (slave), it can maintain 1-2 simultaneous connections depending on connection parameters. Key factors affecting connection capacity: (1) Connection interval - Shorter intervals require more processing time per connection. (2) Data throughput - High data rate connections consume more RAM for buffering. (3) Application processing - Complex application logic reduces available CPU time for BLE stack. (4) Memory usage - Each connection requires approximately 1-2KB RAM for stack buffers. For typical sensor applications with 100ms connection intervals and low data rates, 4-6 simultaneous connections are practical. For applications requiring many connections, consider using a connection manager to rotate between devices or implement a gateway architecture with multiple MM32W devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for multi-connection BLE architecture design and optimization.

faqs.question: Does MM32W373 support BLE mesh networking?
faqs.answer: The MM32W373PF hardware is capable of BLE mesh networking, but mesh support depends on the software stack implementation. The MindMotion standard BLE stack focuses on point-to-point and star topologies. For BLE mesh networking, you have several options: (1) Third-party mesh stack - Integrate a certified BLE mesh stack from vendors like Silicon Labs or Nordic Semiconductor. These stacks implement the full Bluetooth Mesh specification including relay, proxy, friend, and low power node features. (2) Custom mesh implementation - Implement a proprietary mesh protocol on top of the standard BLE advertising and connection features. This provides flexibility but requires significant development effort. (3) Hybrid approach - Use MM32W for BLE connectivity to a mesh gateway device that handles the mesh protocol. For immediate mesh applications requiring standard Bluetooth Mesh compliance, consider using a dedicated mesh solution or external mesh module alongside the MM32W.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for BLE mesh networking options and architecture recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the OTA firmware update capability?
faqs.answer: The MM32W373PF supports secure over-the-air (OTA) firmware updates via BLE: (1) Dual-bank Flash - The 128KB Flash can be partitioned into two 64KB banks, allowing new firmware to be downloaded to the inactive bank while the current firmware runs. (2) DFU service - Standard Device Firmware Update GATT service for firmware image transfer. (3) Security - Firmware images can be encrypted and signed to prevent unauthorized updates. Signature verification ensures only authentic firmware is installed. (4) Resume capability - Interrupted updates can resume from the last successfully transferred block. (5) Rollback protection - Failed updates automatically revert to the previous working firmware. The OTA transfer speed depends on connection interval and PHY mode, typically achieving 2-5KB/s with 2Mbps PHY. A full 64KB firmware update takes approximately 15-30 seconds depending on connection parameters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for OTA implementation guidance and secure update architecture design.

faqs.question: Can MM32W373 act as both peripheral and central simultaneously?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MM32W373PF supports simultaneous peripheral and central roles, enabling flexible network architectures: (1) Peripheral role - Advertises and accepts connections from central devices like smartphones or gateways. Can maintain 1-2 connections as slave. (2) Central role - Scans for and connects to peripheral devices like sensors or beacons. Can maintain up to 8 connections as master. (3) Simultaneous operation - The device can simultaneously be connected as peripheral to a smartphone while acting as central to multiple sensors. This enables gateway functionality where the MM32W aggregates data from multiple sensors and forwards to a smartphone or cloud gateway. (4) Connection management - The application must manage connection priorities, data routing, and power consumption when operating in both roles. This dual-role capability is valuable for IoT gateway applications, smart home hubs, and industrial sensor aggregators.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for dual-role BLE architecture design and implementation guidance.

products.name: MM32W040 Compact BLE MCU
products.shortDescription: Compact BLE 5.0 MCU with 32KB Flash, small QFN32 package for space-constrained wireless applications
descriptionParagraphs: The MM32W040PF is a compact wireless microcontroller featuring ARM Cortex-M0+ core with integrated Bluetooth Low Energy 5.0 radio in a small QFN32 package.
This MCU includes 32KB Flash memory and 4KB SRAM, providing sufficient resources for simple BLE beacon and sensor applications. The small package size is ideal for space-constrained designs.
The device achieves ultra-low power consumption with sub-microamp standby current. It operates from 1.8V to 3.6V and supports industrial temperature range.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wireless Applications
faeReview.content: The MM32W040PF is perfect for space-constrained BLE applications where cost and size are critical. The QFN32 package is significantly smaller than the QFN48 used in MM32W073, saving valuable PCB space. I recommend this for BLE beacons, asset tags, and simple sensor nodes where 32KB Flash is sufficient. The RF performance is slightly reduced compared to MM32W073 (+4dBm vs +8dBm TX power), but still adequate for most short-range applications. For a BLE beacon project, the small size allowed fitting the entire circuit in a compact housing.
faeReview.highlight: Compact QFN32 BLE MCU for space-constrained applications

faqs.question: What is the range difference between MM32W040 and MM32W073?
faqs.answer: The MM32W040PF has +4dBm maximum TX power compared to +8dBm on MM32W073. This 4dB difference translates to approximately 40% less range in free space. In practical terms, expect 15-20 meters indoor range for MM32W040 versus 25-30 meters for MM32W073. For beacon and short-range sensor applications, this range is usually sufficient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MM32W040 for short-range compact designs; use MM32W073 for longer range requirements.

products.name: MM32W580 High-Performance BLE MCU
products.shortDescription: High-performance BLE 5.2 MCU with 256KB Flash, Cortex-M4F core, and advanced security for complex IoT applications
descriptionParagraphs: The MM32W580PF is a high-performance wireless microcontroller featuring ARM Cortex-M4F core with integrated Bluetooth Low Energy 5.2 radio. It enables complex IoT applications requiring advanced processing and security.

This MCU includes 256KB Flash memory and 64KB SRAM with BLE 5.2 features including LE Audio, AoA/AoD direction finding, and enhanced security. The Cortex-M4F core with FPU and DSP instructions provides exceptional processing power.

Advanced security features include hardware cryptographic acceleration, secure boot, and TrustZone technology. The device supports complex applications including voice processing and machine learning at the edge.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wireless Applications
faeReview.content: The MM32W580PF is MindMotion's flagship BLE MCU with the most advanced features. The Cortex-M4F core provides 3-4x the processing power of M0+ devices, enabling complex algorithms and signal processing. The BLE 5.2 features including LE Audio and direction finding open new application possibilities. I recommend this for advanced wearables with audio, location tracking systems using AoA/AoD, and smart home hubs requiring multi-protocol support. The hardware cryptography accelerates secure connections and data encryption. For a location tracking project, the AoA feature enabled sub-meter accuracy with multiple anchor points.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance BLE 5.2 MCU with Cortex-M4F and advanced security features

faqs.question: What is LE Audio and how does it differ from classic Bluetooth audio?
faqs.answer: LE Audio is a new Bluetooth audio standard built on BLE 5.2. Key differences from classic Bluetooth audio: (1) Lower power consumption - Uses BLE instead of classic Bluetooth, extending battery life by 2-3x. (2) Multi-stream - Supports multiple independent audio streams to different devices. (3) Broadcast - Allows one-to-many audio broadcasting. (4) Better quality - LC3 codec provides better audio quality at lower bitrates. (5) Hearing aids - Native support for hearing aid applications. The MM32W580PF's BLE 5.2 radio supports LE Audio, enabling next-generation audio applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MM32W580 for LE Audio applications; use MM32W373 for standard BLE connectivity.
